description:
## Why

The front-end gate currently runs `type_module` twice on every compile:
once on the raw IR for `@spec` validation, once on a reducer-applied
clone for pattern_check's survivor gating. The second pass is expensive
and pattern_check throws away the entire typed result ‚Äî it consumes one
field (`ModuleTypes.reachable_specs`) and immediately collapses it to
`(name, arity)`.

`reachable_specs` isn't a typing fact. It's `specs.keys().collect()` ‚Äî
the set of `(fn, arg-shape)` keys the typer's worklist visited while
walking from `main`. Reachability is a property of the call graph, not
the type lattice. The second `type_module` exists only because
reachability got stapled onto `ModuleTypes`.

On top of that, the gate function `validate_specs_or_exit` fuses three
unrelated concerns: analysis, presentation (render diagnostics), and
control flow (decide to exit). The analyses themselves are pure
(`spec_check::validate_specs`, `pattern_check::check_program` both
return `Vec<Diagnostic>`); the wrapper bolts presentation + exit onto
them and bundles two unrelated checks while doing it.

## Shape

Move reachability off the typer; extract a small call-graph walk.
Decouple the analysis layer from the report-and-exit layer.

Pure checks (analysis):
- `check_specs(prog, module) -> Vec<Diagnostic>`
- `check_patterns(prog, module) -> Vec<Diagnostic>` (clones, reduces,
  walks `reachable_fns`, builds survivor set internally)

Shared sink (presentation + control flow):
- `diag::report_or_exit(diags, sm)` ‚Äî render all, exit 1 if any error.

Driver:
```rust
let mut diags = check_specs(&prog, &module);
diags.extend(check_patterns(&prog, &module));
diag::report_or_exit(&diags, &sm);
```

## What gets removed

- `ModuleTypes.reachable_specs` field + construction at ir_typer.rs:826.
- The parity test `module_types_exposes_reachable_specs` at
  ir_typer_tests.rs:1856 (testing a field that no longer exists).
- One full `type_module` call in the front-end gate (main.rs:73).
- `validate_specs_or_exit` as a name and a shape.
- `build_call_graph` and `entry_seeds` as private ir_typer items ‚Äî
  moved (not deleted) to a shared `ir_callgraph` module so there is one
  source of truth for "what counts as a call edge."

## What gets added

- `pub fn reachable_fns(&Module) -> HashSet<FnId>` in a new
  `src/ir_callgraph.rs`. Pure call-graph BFS from `entry_seeds`. No
  types involved.
- `pub fn diag::report_or_exit(&[Diagnostic], &SourceMap)`.

## Atoms (child tickets)

- **A** ‚Äî Extract `ir_callgraph` module: move `build_call_graph` +
  `entry_seeds` from ir_typer; add `reachable_fns`. Unit tests.
- **B** ‚Äî Dump outcomes filter at main.rs:858 projects from
  `mt.specs.keys()` instead of `mt.reachable_specs`. Independent.
- **C** ‚Äî Pattern_check uses `reachable_fns(&reduced)`; delete the
  inner `type_module(&reduced)`. Depends A. Fixture matrix parity
  required.
- **D** ‚Äî Delete `ModuleTypes.reachable_specs` field, construction,
  parity test. Depends B, C.
- **E1** ‚Äî Add `diag::report_or_exit`. Independent.
- **E2** ‚Äî Replace `validate_specs_or_exit` with pure `check_specs`
  / `check_patterns`; driver sites use `diag::report_or_exit`.
  Depends C, E1.

## Known parity risk (atom C)

Today's `mt.reachable_specs` is *type-narrowed* reachability ‚Äî the
typer's worklist drops specs the produces/holders BFS at
ir_typer.rs:767‚Äì783 can't reach. A pure call-graph walk over the
reduced module is a **strict superset**: it includes any fn
syntactically called in the post-reduce IR, even if the typer would
later prove all its specs unreachable via spec narrowing.

Failure mode: a fn that's textually called but whose every spec is
dead at the type level. Today pattern_check skips ‚Üí no warning. After
C, pattern_check includes it ‚Üí warning fires. This is arguably
*more* correct (if the body is in the post-reduce IR, a
`:function_clause` halt could fire at runtime), but it's a behavior
change. Atom C requires fixture-matrix parity confirmation; any
diffs must be reviewed and either accepted or fixed before merge.

## Out of scope

- Eliminating the clone+reduce inside `check_patterns` by reordering
  pattern_check to piggyback on the driver's own subsequent
  `reduce_module`. Worth doing, separate concern (changes gate timing
  vs codegen).
- Migrating the other ~18 `render_one_to_stderr + exit(1)` sites in
  main.rs to use `diag::report_or_exit`. Filed as fz-NEW.E3 follow-up
  once E1 exists.
- The standalone `ir_typer::reachable_specs(...)` function (line
  2877) ‚Äî different beast (SpecId-level, used by codegen for trap-stub
  gating, seeded from closure_shapes + any-keys + extra_seeds). Stays.

## Supersedes

fz-191 (Unbundle validate_specs_or_exit). The original framing
treated the symptom (a misleading function name) instead of the cause
(reachability on the wrong type). After A‚ÄìD land, E2 becomes
trivial ‚Äî the function split is the natural consequence, not the
target.
title: Collapse front-end's second type pass and decouple check/report
